title: Enhancing Phase Retrievability of Quantum Channels via Interferometric Coupling
authors: Kai Liu, Deguang Han, Omar Nour
abstract: Phase retrievability of a quantum channel asks whether pure states can be reconstructed from suitable measurements. In this paper, we study this problem from three complementary viewpoints: quantum information theory, operator-valued frames, and the physical realization through quantum interferometry. We first show that a quantum channel is phase retrievable if and only if its complementary channel is pure-state informationally complete. This structural characterization leads to several consequences for phase retrievability, including criteria involving the dimension of the complementary operator system, Choi-rank type bounds, and specific results for entanglement breaking channels and twirling channels.   We then introduce an interferometric coupling in which two arm channels are coherently recombined through port operators \(M_i(θ)=A_i+e^{iθ}B_i\). Unlike classical mixing, this construction produces interference cross terms that can enlarge the complementary operator system and thereby enhance phase retrievability. From the frame theory viewpoint, the interferometer realizes a coherent coupling of operator-valued frames. To quantify this effect, we introduce injectivity indices for completely positive maps. The examples in Section~5 show that coherent interference can significantly improve phase retrieval behavior even when the arm channels are individually not phase retrievable.
summary: This paper addresses the problem of phase retrievability in quantum channels, which is the ability to reconstruct pure input states from measurement data. The authors establish a fundamental link between a channel's phase retrievability and the informational completeness of its complementary channel. They propose that using a quantum interferometer to coherently combine two channels can create interference cross-terms that enhance retrievability. This mechanism allows for state reconstruction even when the individual channels used in the interferometer are not themselves phase retrievable.
main_problem: The paper investigates the conditions for phase retrievability of quantum channels and explores whether interferometric coupling can enhance the ability to reconstruct pure input states.
main_result: The authors prove that a channel is phase retrievable if and only if its complementary channel is pure-state informationally complete, and demonstrate that coherent interferometric coupling can enable phase retrieval even when individual arm channels are not phase retrievable.
method: The study utilizes quantum information theory (Stinespring dilation, complementary channels), frame theory (operator-valued Parseval frames), and algebraic analysis of Kraus operators.
model_or_system: A two-arm quantum interferometer where two quantum channels are coherently recombined via port operators M_i(theta) = A_i + e^{i*theta}B_i, representing a Mach-Zehnder type setup.
key_observables: Phase retrievability index (pr), injectivity indices (I and I_av), and the dimension of the complementary operator system.
important_parameters: Interferometric phase (theta), damping parameter (gamma), rotation angle (alpha), and the dimension of the Hilbert space (d).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes finite-dimensional complex Hilbert spaces and focuses primarily on the reconstruction of pure states rather than mixed states.
figures_summary: Schematic of a two-path interferometer and heat maps showing the injectivity index and average CP injectivity as functions of phase and rotation parameters.
paper_structure: The paper begins with a structural characterization of phase retrievability via complementary channels, moves to the introduction of the interferometric coupling model, applies frame theory to analyze the enhancement mechanism, and concludes with specific examples and quantitative injectivity analysis.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to quantum optics and open quantum systems as it provides a fundamental framework for using coherent interference to recover information that is otherwise lost to the environment.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
